titleStmt.title: SII 28.289: original edition by J. Sundaram
No. 289. (A.R. No. 443 of 1913.)

summary.p: Kāmēśvara temple, second prākāra, west wall, left of entrance.

Records a royal order addressed to the tāṉattār of the temple of Tirukkāmīśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār of Āragaḷūr, granting 3500 kuḻi measured by vaṉṉenjaṉ-kōl of first grade wet land irrigated by the big reservoir (periya-ēri) in Alampaḷam in Naraiyūr-kūṟṟam in Milāḍu alias Jaṉaṉātha-vaḷanāḍu, and [1]5,000 kuḻi of dry-crop-land, out of land held by the king (nam-paṟṟu), as tirunāmattukkāṇi (land directly held by the God/temple), to serve as capital to provide the paddy and cash required for providing food-offerings and decoration to the God during the service Ulagu-muḻuduḍaiyār-sandhi instituted in the name of the king (for the God of their temple). The temple-authorities (tāṉattār) could enlist kuḍis (for working the land) as they pleased and could raise any crop according to their wish during the cultivation seasons, kār (rain-fed) and paśāṉam. The king agreed to forego the levies kaḍamai, poṉ-vari, kāśāyam, kāttikaippachchai,mātiri sānti nall-erudu, naṟ-paśu, as due from that land. The right to one half of the maintenance granted to the one appointed to write the accounts of the ūr, constituting the āḷ-amañji-tēvai, echōṟu and such in respect of that land could also be collected and utilised. The rights, nā-ūrumai and kālurimai in respect of that land were also authorised to be enjoyed. The record was attested by [Chiṟinaṅgū]ruḍaiyāṉ.

One noteworthy feature in the record is that recording of the extent of dry-crop-land has been tampered with. What was originally 5000 kuḻi has been altered to 15,000 by correcting the word kuḻi to padi and the total extent of land was also made into 18500 kuḻi.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . Aruḷicceyal Āṟakaḷur Uṭaiyār tirukāmicuramuṭai
ya nāyaṉār koyil tāṉattārkku nam perāl kaṭṭiṉa Ulakumuḻutuṭaiyār ca
ntikku Amutupaṭi cāttumpaṭi Uḷḷiṭṭa nimantaṅkaḷukku tāṅkaḷ Iṭṭa Uṭaṟkoḷḷumpaṭi nel
lukkum paṇattukkum Uṭalāka nam paṟṟāṉa milāṭākiya jaṉaṉātavaḷanāṭu naraiyūr kūṟṟattu Alampaḷattil pe
riya Erivārappaṟṟu naṉcai nilattu mutal tarattil taṭico paḻavoḻukiṉapaṭi vaṉṉeñcaṉ Aḷavukolāl naṉ
cai kuḻi muvāyirattu Aiññāṟum puṉcai patiHere as first kuḻi was written and then corrected to patiAiyyāyiramum Āka naṉcai puṉcai kuḻi patieṇṇāHere also eṇṇāyirattu seen to have been corrected to patieṇṇāyirattuyirattu Aiñnū r nam toppu
Aṭaṅkalum Iṉta nila tirunāmattuk kāṇiyāka kaikkoṇṭu tāṉ veṇṭum kuṭikku Aṭaittu kārpacāṉam Uḷḷi
ṭṭu tāṅkaḷ veṇṭum payircceytu koṇṭu Iṉṉilattukku kaṭamai poṉvari kācāyam kāttikaippaccai mātiri
cānti nallerutu naṟpacu Ūr kaṇakka jivitaṅ Oṉṟupāti Āḷamañci tevai Eṟcoṟu Uḷḷiṭṭa maṟṟum Eṟperppaṭ
ṭa Aṉaittāyamu Uppaṭa Iḻantu Iṉṉilattāl varum nā Urumai kāl Urumai maṟṟum Epperppaṭṭa Urimaikaḷum Uppaṭa mutal Aṭa
ṅkilum Iṟaiyili tirunāmattukkāṇiyāka cantirātittavaraiyum celvatāka kuṭuttom Ippaṭikku Ivvolai piṭipāṭā
ka koṇṭu kallilum cempilum veṭṭi tiruccūlattāpaṉamum paṇṇikkoḷka Ivai ciṟinaṅkūruṭaiyāṉ Eḻuttu tulyam māheśvara rakṣai .
Digital edition of SII 28.289 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
